question: Write a C++ program with functions for array operations: 1) InputArray to get array elements from user, 2) DisplayArray to show array elements, 3) FindMax to return maximum element, 4) FindMin to return minimum element, 5) CalculateSum to return sum of all elements. Use these functions to process an array of integers.

test_cases.expected: Enter numbers (enter -1 to stop): You entered: 1 2 3 4 5
Enter numbers (enter -1 to stop): You entered: 10 20

errors.description: Poor formatting and naming: Multiple variable declarations on one line with cryptic names 'a', 's', 'c', 'x'.
Entire control flow compressed into single line, making code extremely hard to read and debug.
Complex array expansion logic compressed into one line, severely reducing maintainability and readability.
Output logic and loops compressed into single lines without proper spacing or structure.
